title: Publications | Stats NZ
content:
## He maha atu ngā tāngata e piri ana ki te momo iwi Māori
Tata ki te 932,000 tāngata, arā, 18 ōrau o te taupori e noho ana i Aotearoa i te piri ki te momo iwi Māori i te 30 Hune 2025.

## More people identifying with Māori ethnicity
Around 932,000 people, or 18 percent of the population living in Aotearoa New Zealand, identified with Māori ethnicity as at 30 June 2025.

## Māori population estimates: At 30 June 2025
Māori population estimates give the best available measure of the Māori ethnic population, by age and sex, usually living in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## National population estimates: At 30 September 2025
National population estimates give the best available measure of the population, by age and sex, usually living in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## Births and deaths: Year ended September 2025
Births and deaths releases provide statistics on the number of births and deaths of people resident in New Zealand that are registered during a given period, along with selected fertility and mortality rates. They may differ from statistics presented elsewhere that relate to all births and deaths registered in New Zealand or to births and deaths occurring during a given period.

## Net migration gain of 12,400
New Zealand had a net migration gain of 12,400 in the September 2025 year, down from a net gain of 42,400 in the September 2024 year.

## International migration: September 2025
International migration statistics give the latest outcomes-based measure of migration, which includes estimates of migrants entering or leaving New Zealand.

## Australian visitor arrivals increase
Visitor arrivals from Australia reached 1.48 million in the September 2025 year, up from 1.33 million the year before.

## International travel: September 2025
International travel covers the number and characteristics of overseas visitors and New Zealand resident travellers (short-term movements) entering or leaving New Zealand.

## Population growth slows in all regions
Population growth slowed in all of Aotearoa New Zealand’s 16 regions in the year ended June 2025.

## Subnational population estimates: At 30 June 2025
Subnational population estimates give the best available measure of the population, by age and sex, usually living in New Zealand's 16 regions, 67 territorial authority areas, 21 Auckland local board areas, and other areas.

## International travel: August 2025
International travel covers the number and characteristics of overseas visitors and New Zealand resident travellers (short-term movements) entering or leaving New Zealand.
